prGroups.title: Transport wiring: SW inbound validation + SW→panel Port + panel focus store

tasks.text: Extend SwToPanel in src/shared/messages.ts with two new variants that let the SW forward content-originated picker events to the panel: `focus` (selector: StableSelector, rect: Rect) forwarded from ContentToSw.element-picked, and `picker-state` (active: boolean) forwarded from ContentToSw.picker-state. Both types already have precedent in ContentToSw; the SW acts as a relay. Then create src/shared/port.ts exporting: (a) PORT_NAME constant (e.g. 'dz-sw-panel') referenced by both SW onConnect and panel connect, (b) parseSwToPanel(raw): SwToPanel | null — a Zod-safeParse wrapper that returns null on malformed input so the receiver never trusts an unshaped Port message (mirrors the PanelToSw validation pattern in background.ts). Keep the module chrome-free (pure Zod + constant only). filesHint: src/shared/messages.ts, src/shared/port.ts

In src/entrypoints/background.ts: (1) Import PORT_NAME from @/shared/port and ContentToSw + SwToPanel type from @/shared/messages. (2) Add a chrome.runtime.onConnect listener that checks port.name === PORT_NAME, stores the Port in a Set<chrome.runtime.Port>, and removes it onDisconnect. (3) Add a postToPanel(msg: SwToPanel) helper that iterates the live Ports and postMessage's each. (4) Add a SECOND chrome.runtime.onMessage listener (separate from the existing PanelToSw listener) that does ContentToSw.safeParse(raw); if it fails, return early (the PanelToSw listener handles panel messages — no type-literal overlap exists between the two unions). On success, switch on type: element-picked → postToPanel({ type: 'focus', selector: msg.candidates[0], rect: msg.rect }) guarding for an empty candidates array; picker-state → postToPanel({ type: 'picker-state', active: msg.active }); multi-select-changed and recorder-event are no-op stubs with TODO comments (their consumers land in later issues). This listener does NOT call sendResponse (content→SW push is fire-and-forget forwarding, not RPC). Do NOT modify the existing PanelToSw listener or the handle() switch. filesHint: src/entrypoints/background.ts

Three panel-side changes forming the panel transport layer. (1) In src/entrypoints/sidepanel/stores/bus.ts: add connectPort() (guarded by a `connected` boolean so it's idempotent — opens chrome.runtime.connect({ name: PORT_NAME }), wires port.onMessage through parseSwToPanel, dispatches validated SwToPanel to a Set of subscribers) and subscribeToSw(handler: (msg: SwToPanel) => void) returning an unsubscribe function. Keep the existing request() RPC function unchanged. (2) Create src/entrypoints/sidepanel/stores/focus.ts: a SolidJS signal-based focus store tracking { selector: StableSelector | null, rect: Rect | null, pickerActive: boolean }. Export a PURE reduceFocus(state: FocusState, msg: SwToPanel): FocusState function (so it's unit-testable without chrome mocks) that handles `focus` (set selector+rect) and `picker-state` (set pickerActive; on inactive also clear selector+rect). Then export initFocusStore() which calls connectPort() + subscribeToSw, routing messages through reduceFocus into the signals. Export clearFocus() for manual reset. (3) In src/entrypoints/sidepanel/main.tsx: call initFocusStore() before render (after initSentry). filesHint: src/entrypoints/sidepanel/stores/bus.ts, src/entrypoints/sidepanel/stores/focus.ts, src/entrypoints/sidepanel/main.tsx

Two test files. (1) Extend test/unit/messages.test.ts: add a describe block for the new SwToPanel variants — assert SwToPanel.safeParse accepts a well-formed `focus` (with a valid StableSelector + Rect) and `picker-state` { active: true }, rejects `focus` missing rect, rejects `picker-state` with non-boolean active, and confirms both are members of the SwToPanel union (use the existing selector/rect fixtures already defined at the top of the file). (2) Create test/unit/focus.test.ts: unit-test the pure reduceFocus reducer — starts with null selector/null rect/inactive picker; a `focus` message sets selector+rect; a subsequent `picker-state { active: false }` clears them and sets pickerActive false; `picker-state { active: true }` sets pickerActive true; non-focus messages (token, error, etc.) are no-ops (state unchanged). No chrome mocking needed — reduceFocus is pure. These are unit tests under test/unit/ so they run with `bun run test:unit` and `bun test`. filesHint: test/unit/messages.test.ts, test/unit/focus.test.ts
